summary: Implemented the first concrete DinoBrain OS gap fixes from the user document comparison: stronger full-memory audit metadata, status freshness authority metadata, conservative direct MCP parity status, higher-level OS health rollup, task lifecycle repair application, and verification documentation. App repo commit c29e92e was pushed to GitHub.
decisions: Do not mark OS health green when direct Codex/Claude MCP parity proof is missing.
Treat status freshness as currentness, and health status as semantic OS readiness.
Full-memory audit now records UTF-8/binary/non-UTF8 class plus text size and parse metadata so drift can be classified without schema-upgrade false positives.
Applied task lifecycle settlement for a blocked task missing terminal trace as blocked evidence, not successful completion evidence.
verify:goal remains blocked only on real Codex Desktop live pre-response evidence after stale MCP process cleanup.
next_steps: Fully restart Codex, paste the live-proof prompt into a fresh Codex Desktop workspace thread, then rerun npm run verify:goal.
Implement exact single-name direct MCP proof ingestion for Codex and Claude so client_mcp_direct_status can move from needs_recheck to verified.
Keep data repo sync scoped; it currently has broad pre-existing candidate/review churn and should not be committed wholesale.
search_queries: DinoBrain OS implementable improvement plan comparison
full-memory audit status freshness health status direct MCP parity
task lifecycle settlement finish gate integrity
